Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrates. glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable per Veritas protocol. hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat content (~10.8 g/100g) contributing to arterial stiffness, partially offset by low sodium (56 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is low at 56 mg/100g, which is favourable for blood pressure management; however, the high saturated fat content (~10.8 g/100g) may contribute to arterial stiffness and endothelial dysfunction, so individuals with hypertension should trim visible fat, avoid salt-heavy marinades, and limit portion size to one chop per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates eliminate direct glycaemic impact, but high saturated fat (~10.8 g/100g) may worsen insulin resistance with habitual consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled pork chop contains zero carbohydrates and will not cause a direct postprandial glucose spike, making it suitable for glycaemic control in the short term; however, habitual consumption of high saturated fat (~10.8 g/100g) may worsen insulin resistance over time, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should choose leaner cuts, trim visible fat, and avoid sweet glazes or sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (30.1 g/100g) and estimated saturated fat (~10.8 g/100g) exceeding recommended limits for cardiovascular risk reduction
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high total fat (30.1 g/100g) and saturated fat (~10.8 g/100g) content of grilled pork chop significantly exceed cardiovascular-protective thresholds; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption, choose leaner pork cuts such as tenderloin, trim all visible fat before grilling, and avoid high-fat accompaniments to reduce LDL-cholesterol burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~10.8 g/100g) exceeding the 5 g/100g RA threshold and arachidonic acid content amplifying inflammatory cytokines
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Grilled pork chop is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its high saturated fat content (~10.8 g/100g) and arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplify pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways; consumption should be limited, all visible fat trimmed, and paired with omega-3-rich foods to partially offset the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled pork chop is a minimally processed meat dish consisting of a pork midloin chop cooked directly over a grill or griddle without breading or batter. The midloin cut is taken from the loin section of the pig and retains a moderate fat cap that renders during grilling, producing a juicy interior with a lightly charred, savoury exterior. The texture is firm yet tender when cooked to the recommended internal temperature of 63°C (145°F). Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 363 kcal, 23.7 g protein, 30.1 g total fat (estimated saturated fat ~10.8 g), and 0.0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 56 mg/100g. Calcium is 14 mg/100g and iron is 1.0 mg/100g. The glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content. Clinically, the zero-carbohydrate profile is favourable for glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes, but the high saturated fat content warrants caution for cardiovascular disease, hypertension, and rheumatoid arthritis. It is gluten-free and dairy-free. Nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F06100015, confidence 0.95); saturated fat adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 10062.

NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: sugar_g is set to -1 because the HPB Singapore Foodo source (cr_id F06100015) does not report sugar content for this grilled pork midloin chop preparation. dietary_fibre_g is set to -1 because the HPB Singapore Foodo source does not report dietary fibre for this item. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific grilled pork midloin chop preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026. 2. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: carbohydrate_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F06100015); grilled pork midloin chop is naturally zero in carbohydrates as it is a plain grilled meat with no added carbohydrate-containing ingredients. 3. SATURATED FAT ADAPTATION: saturated_fat_g (~10.8 g/100g) is adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork loin chop cooked grilled (NDB 10062), as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown. This is an adapted value and should be verified against updated HPB data when available. 4. GI NOT APPLICABLE: Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g per HPB Singapore Foodo). glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable per Veritas protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. 5. HALAL STATUS: Pork is not halal; is_halal is set to false. 6. MULTILINGUAL NAMES: This is a Western generic food item with no mandatory non-English local-language names required under G6 multilingual rules.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record F06100015 (Grilled Pork Chop) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on iteration 3. Both are resolved: gi_source now correctly uses source_match_type 'not_applicable' with organization 'N/A', and glycemic_index is null per the zero-carbohydrate protocol. Nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (primary approved source, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and documented. All -1 sentinel values documented. Zero carbohydrate justified as naturally zero and confirmed by HPB. Ontology anchored with defensible FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Alias consistency verified.
